CONTENT.Early life: Epassy was born and raised in France and now resides in Saudi Arabia.
CONTENT.Club career: Epassy joined Levadiakos on 14 August 2017 from Épinal.On 29 January 2020, Epassy joined Lamia from Levadiakos on a three-year deal.On 15 July 2021, Epassy agreed to join Greek Super League club OFI on a two-year deal. On 18 July 2021, OFI officially announced Epassy after signing a two-year contract with the club, as previously agreed.On 6 August 2022, Epassy joined Saudi Arabian club Abha on a free transfer.
CONTENT.External links: PAS Lamia 1964 - Official websiteSuper League Greece Player Profile - Devis Epassy
CONTENT.International career: Born in France, Epassy is Cameroonian by descent. He was called up to represent the Cameroon national team in June 2021. He debuted with Cameroon in a 0–0 friendly tie with Nigeria on 8 June 2021. He made his first appearance of the 2022 FIFA World Cup in 3–3 tie to Serbia in the Group stage of the tournament after original starting goalkeeper André Onana was sent home by manager Rigobert Song following a disagreement between the two of them. Epassy then kept a clean sheet by producing a total of 7 saves in Cameroon's final game, a 1–0 win against perennial favorites, Brazil.

SUMMARY: Devis Rogers Epassy Mboka (born 2 February 1993) is a professional footballer who plays as a goalkeeper. Born in France, he represents Cameroon at international level.
